title: Mentor Guide — Day 1: Identity & Purpose
coachingObjective: Connect the SPIKE opportunity to each participant's personal ambition. Help them find authentic voice in identity builders — not perfect words.

timing.preSession: 15 min — review squad roster, read Faculty Deck 01/02 speaker notes, print observation form
timing.duringSession: Circulate during builders and squad activities; 5 min intro per squad
timing.postSession: 20 min — complete observation forms and participant snapshots

discussionQuestions: What attracted you to this opportunity?
What future are you hoping to create?
What concerns do you have about the entrepreneurship path?
What part of today resonated most — AIA story, agency story, or your own ambition?
Say your ambition in one sentence — I want to hear it in your words.
Who do you want to help — and why them?
What value will you never compromise?

lookFor: Speaks up in squad without prompting

warningSigns: Generic answers with no personal detail

lookFor: Connects AIA mission to personal why
warningSigns: No connection between ambition and impact
Copied phrases from slides or peers

coachingTips: Listen for authentic voice, not perfect words — specificity beats polish on Day 1.
Ask participants to read ambition aloud before they submit the builder.
Link every coaching comment to Portfolio Identity or Business Plan Chapter 1.
Celebrate one brave moment per participant — naming a fear counts.
Flag participants who did not complete reflection for faculty follow-up.
Do not prescribe career direction — explore both tracks with curiosity.
warningSigns: Copying facilitator or peer language verbatim in builders
Unable to articulate personal ambition when asked directly
Withdrawal from squad dream board or charter activity
Completing builders with one-word or placeholder answers
reflectionPrompts: Write one sentence that captures who you are becoming.
What will you do differently tomorrow because of today's identity work?
expectedOutcomes: Participant Snapshot — mentor can describe participant ambition, impact, and values in own words
Squad charter signed with mentor awareness of roles
Observation form completed for each assigned participant
At-risk participants flagged for Day 2 follow-up

activityCoachingNotes.note: Stand beside participant during coach conversation. If draft sounds generic, ask: 'What would your best friend say you are building?'
Push for one named audience. 'Families' is too broad — 'young parents in BPO' is coachable.
Future self should be present tense. Model one sentence if they are stuck.
Gallery walk — ask each member to explain one image connection to ambition.
Ensure every member has a named role. Conflict norms matter for Week 1 research.

debriefWithFaculty: Which participants need extra support before Day 2?
Did squad formation produce balanced teams?
Any identity outputs that are portfolio-ready vs need rework?
